bali.title: Bali
bali.name: Bali
bali.description: Indonesia's most famous island, known for its forested volcanic mountains, iconic rice paddies, beaches, and coral reefs. A spiritual heartland with thousands of ancient temples.
bali.currency: IDR (Indonesian Rupiah). 1 USD ≈ 16,000 IDR. Credit cards accepted at tourist areas; carry cash for markets and warungs.
bali.language: Bahasa Indonesia (official), Balinese (local). English widely spoken in tourist areas.
bali.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) available for most nationalities at DPS airport — USD 35, valid 30 days, extendable once for another 30 days.
bali.bestTime: April–October (dry season). Avoid January–March for the interior — wet season brings daily rain.
localTips: Dress modestly when visiting temples — a sarong is required. Many temples provide them at the entrance.
Negotiate at markets — starting price is usually 2-3x the fair price.
Rent a scooter (IDR 70,000–100,000/day) or hire a private driver (IDR 450,000–600,000/day).
Avoid Kuta and Seminyak if you want authentic Bali — stay in Ubud, Sidemen, or Amed.
The Bali Spirit Festival (April) and Nyepi (Balinese New Year, March) are unmissable cultural events.

jakarta.title: Jakarta
jakarta.name: Jakarta
jakarta.description: Indonesia's sprawling capital and economic hub, home to 10 million people, world-class malls, vibrant street food scene, and colonial Dutch architecture in Kota Tua.
jakarta.currency: IDR. ATMs everywhere. Most hotels and malls accept Visa/Mastercard.
jakarta.language: Bahasa Indonesia. English spoken in business districts and upscale restaurants.
jakarta.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) for most nationalities — USD 35, valid 30 days.
jakarta.bestTime: May–September (dry season). Jakarta is hot and humid year-round.
localTips: Traffic is legendary — use the MRT or TransJakarta BRT to get around.
Kota Tua (Old Town) is worth half a day — Dutch colonial buildings, Wayang Museum, and Café Batavia.
Street food around Menteng and Glodok is outstanding — try soto betawi and kerak telor.
The National Museum is excellent for understanding Indonesian history and culture.
Grab and Gojek apps are essential for transport and food delivery.

yogyakarta.title: Yogyakarta
yogyakarta.name: Yogyakarta
yogyakarta.description: The cultural heart of Java, home to the ancient temples of Borobudur and Prambanan, the Sultan's Palace (Kraton), and a thriving batik and wayang puppet tradition.
yogyakarta.currency: IDR. Carry cash — many warungs and local markets are cash-only.
yogyakarta.language: Bahasa Indonesia, Javanese. English spoken at tourist sites.
yogyakarta.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
yogyakarta.bestTime: April–October. Borobudur sunrise visits (May–September) offer clear mountain views.
localTips: Wake up at 4am for the Borobudur sunrise tour — it's the most magical experience in Java.
Rent a bicycle in the city — Yogyakarta's downtown is flat and bike-friendly.
Buy batik from Beringharjo Market, not tourist shops — prices are a fraction of the cost.
Visit Prambanan at sunset for dramatic photos of the Hindu temple complex.
The Kraton has free cultural performances on certain mornings.

ubud.title: Ubud
ubud.name: Ubud
ubud.description: Bali's cultural capital, set amid jungle-covered hills and terraced rice paddies. Famous for its vibrant arts scene, cooking classes, yoga retreats, and access to sacred temples.
ubud.airport: Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS) — approximately 1 hour by car.
ubud.currency: IDR. Most restaurants and shops accept cards; carry cash for market shopping.
ubud.language: Balinese, Bahasa Indonesia. English widely spoken in tourist establishments.
ubud.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) at DPS — USD 35, valid 30 days.
ubud.bestTime: April–October. Rice paddies are most lush in November–December.
localTips: The Campuhan Ridge Walk at sunrise is free and spectacular — start before 7am.
Attend a Kecak fire dance at Uluwatu Temple (sunset) — one of the most theatrical performances in Asia.
Tegallalang Rice Terraces are beautiful but crowded — visit before 8am or after 4pm.
Cooking classes at Casa Luna or Paon are outstanding — half-day classes include market visits.
Side streets near the Monkey Forest are quieter and cheaper than Jalan Raya Ubud.

lombok.title: Lombok
lombok.name: Lombok
lombok.description: Bali's quieter neighbor, offering pristine beaches, the sacred volcano Mount Rinjani, and the laid-back Gili Islands just offshore. Sasak culture distinct from Bali.
lombok.currency: IDR. ATMs in Mataram and Senggigi; carry cash for beach areas.
lombok.language: Bahasa Indonesia, Sasak. English in tourist areas.
lombok.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
localTips: The Rinjani crater rim trek (2–3 days) is one of Southeast Asia's best hikes.
Kuta Lombok has excellent surf breaks and is far less developed than Kuta Bali.
Try ayam taliwang — Lombok's famous chili chicken — at any local warung.
Fast boats to the Gili Islands depart from Bangsal Harbor.
Friday Mosque prayers in Mataram are a beautiful cultural experience.

komodo.title: Komodo
komodo.name: Komodo
komodo.description: Home to the world's largest lizard, Komodo National Park encompasses volcanic islands, pristine coral reefs, and pink sand beaches rarely found elsewhere on Earth.
komodo.airport: Komodo Airport (LBJ) in Labuan Bajo, Flores
komodo.currency: IDR. Very limited ATMs in Labuan Bajo — withdraw cash in Bali before arriving.
komodo.language: Bahasa Indonesia. Limited English outside Labuan Bajo town.
komodo.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
localTips: You must pay a Komodo National Park fee — rangers accompany all Komodo dragon viewing.
Pink Beach (Pantai Merah) is genuinely pink — best colors in morning light.
Liveaboard diving trips are the best way to experience the park's remote dive sites.
Budget IDR 600,000–1,500,000 per person for a day boat tour from Labuan Bajo.
Book accommodation in Labuan Bajo well in advance for June to September.

raja-ampat.title: Raja Ampat
raja-ampat.name: Raja Ampat
raja-ampat.description: The crown jewel of Indonesian diving, Raja Ampat's 1,500 islands contain the highest marine biodiversity on Earth. Hidden lagoons, overwater bungalows, and absolute silence.
raja-ampat.airport: DEO Airport in Sorong, West Papua — then 2–4 hour speedboat to Waisai.
raja-ampat.currency: IDR. Very limited banking — bring all cash from Sorong.
raja-ampat.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days. Entry permit for Raja Ampat (IDR 500,000) required.
raja-ampat.bestTime: October–April (best visibility). May–September for pelagic encounters.
localTips: Pay the Raja Ampat Marine Park entry fee (IDR 500,000/person, valid 1 year) at Waisai.
The Pianemo viewpoint hike offers the iconic Raja Ampat vista — go at 7am before day-trippers.
Resorts handle all boat logistics — do not navigate independently without local knowledge.
Bring reef-safe sunscreen only — the marine park is strictly monitored.
Misool, Wayag, and Dampier Strait are the three must-do areas.

bandung.title: Bandung
bandung.tagline: Java's Fashion Capital & Cool Climate Escape
bandung.name: Bandung
bandung.description: Java's fashion capital and a cool-climate escape from Jakarta's heat. Known for its factory outlets, creative food scene, Dutch colonial architecture, and volcanic landscapes nearby.
bandung.airport: Husein Sastranegara Airport (BDO) or Bandung Kertajati Airport (KJT)
bandung.currency: IDR. Cards accepted at malls; cash for street food and factory outlets.
bandung.language: Bahasa Indonesia, Sundanese. English in commercial areas.
bandung.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
bandung.bestTime: April–October. Bandung is pleasantly cool (18–28°C) year-round.
localTips: Factory outlets on Jalan Cihampelas offer brand-name clothing at 30–70% off retail.
The Tangkuban Perahu volcanic crater is 30km north — best visited in the morning before mist rolls in.
Bandung's café culture is exceptional — the Dago area has dozens of themed specialty coffee shops.
Travel Jakarta to Bandung by KAI Intercity train — faster and more comfortable than driving.
Try nasi timbel and batagor at the Braga area food stalls.

surabaya.title: Surabaya
surabaya.name: Surabaya
surabaya.description: Indonesia's second-largest city, a gritty, energetic port town with historic Arab Quarter, impressive colonial architecture, and gateway to Mount Bromo and Ijen Crater.
surabaya.currency: IDR. Full banking infrastructure. Cards accepted widely.
surabaya.language: Bahasa Indonesia, Javanese. English in business districts.
surabaya.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
surabaya.bestTime: April–October. Mount Bromo is accessible year-round.
localTips: Surabaya is best used as a base for Mount Bromo (4-hour drive) and Ijen Crater (6-hour drive).
The Arab Quarter (Ampel) near Sunan Ampel Mosque is atmospheric — visit in the afternoon.
Rawon (black beef soup) and lontong balap are Surabayan street food classics.
The House of Sampoerna tobacco museum is free and fascinating.
Arrange private drivers for Bromo and Ijen through your hotel.

malang.title: Malang
malang.name: Malang
malang.description: A charming colonial town in East Java's highlands, with a cool climate, Dutch-era mansions, colorful painted villages, and easy access to Mount Bromo and Semeru.
malang.currency: IDR. ATMs plentiful. Cash preferred at local warungs.
malang.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
malang.bestTime: April–October. Cool highland climate makes it comfortable year-round.
localTips: Kampung Warna-Warni (colorful village) is photogenic — visit in the afternoon light.
The Ijen Blue Fire (best seen 2–4am) is one of the world's most otherworldly natural phenomena.
Malang's Kasin area has some of the best colonial architecture outside of Bandung.
Apple orchards in the nearby Batu area offer pick-your-own experiences from April–July.
Bakso (meatball soup) is a Malang specialty — try it at Bakso President.

flores.title: Flores
flores.name: Flores
flores.description: A rugged volcanic island of extraordinary beauty, from the tri-colored crater lakes of Kelimutu to traditional Ngada villages, whale watching, and gateway to Komodo.
flores.airport: Komodo Airport (LBJ) in Labuan Bajo; H. Hasan Aroeboesman Airport (ENE) in Ende.
flores.currency: IDR. Very limited ATMs outside major towns — carry cash.
flores.language: Bahasa Indonesia. Multiple local languages (Manggarai, Ngada, Ende).
flores.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
flores.bestTime: May–October. Dry conditions needed for the Trans-Flores Highway.
localTips: The Trans-Flores Highway is one of Indonesia's great overland journeys — allow 4–5 days minimum.
Kelimutu's three crater lakes change color unpredictably — arrive before 6am.
Bena Village near Bajawa is one of Flores' best-preserved traditional villages.
Whale sharks and manta rays are seen year-round in Labuan Bajo's bay.
Ikat weaving is Flores' most prized handicraft — each village has its own distinct patterns.

gili-islands.title: Gili Islands
gili-islands.tagline: No Cars, Just Coral and Cocktails
gili-islands.name: Gili Islands
gili-islands.description: Three tiny coral-fringed islands off Lombok's northwest coast — famous for sea turtles, no motorized vehicles, and sunset cocktails on Gili Trawangan.
gili-islands.airport: Lombok International Airport (LOP) — then 45-minute boat from Bangsal.
gili-islands.currency: IDR. ATMs on Gili Trawangan only; carry cash.
gili-islands.language: Bahasa Indonesia, Sasak. English widely spoken.
gili-islands.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
gili-islands.bestTime: May–October. Sea is calmest and visibility best for snorkeling.
localTips: Gili T is the party island. Gili Air is the social-but-calm middle ground. Gili Meno is for honeymooners.
Snorkeling with turtles is virtually guaranteed at Turtle Point between Gili Meno and Gili Air.
No motorbikes or cars on any of the Gilis — cidomos and bicycles only.
The western beach strip on Gili T is the best sunset spot — arrive 30 minutes early.
Fast boats from Bali connect directly to the Gilis — book through Gili Getaway or Scoot.

nusa-penida.title: Nusa Penida
nusa-penida.name: Nusa Penida
nusa-penida.description: Bali's dramatic southern neighbor, known for sheer clifftop viewpoints, crystal-clear snorkeling with manta rays, and raw beauty in sharp contrast to polished Bali.
nusa-penida.airport: Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS) — then 45-minute fast boat from Sanur.
nusa-penida.currency: IDR. Limited ATMs — bring sufficient cash from Bali.
nusa-penida.language: Balinese, Bahasa Indonesia. Limited English outside tourist areas.
nusa-penida.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
nusa-penida.bestTime: April–October. Roads are rough — 4WD or confident scooter riding necessary.
localTips: Kelingking Beach viewpoint is stunning but the descent is extremely steep — only attempt with good fitness.
Manta Point snorkeling (year-round mantas) and Crystal Bay are must-dos.
Hire a driver (IDR 400,000–600,000/day) unless you're confident on rough roads.
Bring all medications and supplies from Bali — healthcare on the island is very limited.
Diamond Beach and Atuh Beach on the south coast reward those who venture beyond the Instagram spots.

lake-toba.title: Lake Toba
lake-toba.name: Lake Toba
lake-toba.description: The world's largest volcanic lake, formed by a supervolcanic eruption 74,000 years ago. At its center sits Samosir Island, home to Batak culture and extraordinary traditional architecture.
lake-toba.airport: Silangit International Airport (DTB) or Kualanamu International Airport (KNO) near Medan.
lake-toba.currency: IDR. ATMs in Parapat and Samosir towns. Carry cash for villages.
lake-toba.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
lake-toba.bestTime: May–September. The lake region is cooler than coastal Sumatra.
localTips: Stay on Samosir Island (accessible by ferry from Parapat) for the best cultural experience.
Tomok Village has ancient Batak kings' tombs and a traditional market.
Batak music performances at local restaurants in Tuk Tuk are authentic and wonderful.
Rent a motorbike and circumnavigate Samosir Island in a day — the road is flat and views spectacular.
Huta Siallagan stone chairs (tribal court site) are genuinely fascinating.

medan.title: Medan
medan.name: Medan
medan.description: Sumatra's largest city and gateway to Lake Toba, Bukit Lawang orangutan sanctuary, and the Acehnese highlands. Famous for its extraordinary multi-ethnic food scene.
medan.currency: IDR. Full banking infrastructure. Cards accepted widely in the city.
medan.language: Bahasa Indonesia, Batak, Malay, Chinese dialects. Some English in business areas.
medan.visa: Visa on Arrival (VoA) — USD 35, valid 30 days.
medan.bestTime: Year-round. May–September slightly drier. Always hot and humid (25–33°C).
localTips: Medan's food scene is arguably Indonesia's best — must-try: soto Medan, bihun bebek, and nasi goreng Acehnese.
The Grand Mosque (Masjid Raya) and Maimun Palace are Medan's finest landmarks.
Bukit Lawang (4 hours by road) offers ethical orangutan trekking in Gunung Leuser National Park.
Tjong A Fie Mansion is a beautifully preserved Chinese merchant's home — guided tours available.
Medan is genuinely gritty and chaotic — embrace it.
